channel.title: Iac on Anatole HAGBE | DevOps & Cloud Engineer
channel.description: Recent content in Iac on Anatole HAGBE | DevOps & Cloud Engineer

item.title: Writing Terraform Modules That Don't Suck
item.author: ahagbes89@gmail.com (Anatole HAGBE)
item.description:
I’ve read a lot of Terraform that made me want to close my laptop and go work at a coffee shop. Over the years, I’ve collected patterns that separate good modules from painful ones.

The anatomy of a good module

A well-structured Terraform module looks like this:

module/
├── main.tf # Core resources
├── variables.tf # Input variables with validation
├── outputs.tf # Useful outputs
├── versions.tf # Provider version constraints
├── README.md # Auto-generated with terraform-docs
└── examples/
 └── basic/ # Working example

This isn’t revolutionary — it’s what terraform-docs and the Terraform registry expect.

item.title: Terraform AWS Baseline
item.author: ahagbes89@gmail.com (Anatole HAGBE)
item.description:
An open-source Terraform module set that bootstraps a secure AWS multi-account organization from scratch, following AWS Well-Architected Framework guidelines.

Modules Included

  • Organization — AWS Organizations + SSO setup
  • Networking — VPC, subnets, Transit Gateway, VPN
  • Security — GuardDuty, Security Hub, Config, CloudTrail
  • IAM — Permission sets, SCPs, IAM Identity Center
  • Logging — Centralized log archive account
  • Monitoring — CloudWatch alarms + SNS baseline

Tech Stack

Terraform AWS Python GitHub Actions Terratest
